channel.title: Every
channel.description: The only subscription you need to stay at the edge of AI. Ideas, apps, and training.

item.title: After Automation
item.description: AI progress creates more work for humans, not less

item.title: From Doing to Tending
item.description: Plus: A mini-Vibe Check of Grok 4.5, and why AI scribes could dull doctors' judgment

item.title: How GPT-5.6 Changes Knowledge Work
item.description: Don’t do your work. Tend your loop.

item.title: Vibe Check: GPT-5.6 Sol Is Our Favorite Model to Collaborate With
item.description: Sol is fast, resourceful, and unusually easy to steer—but Fable still gets the assignments we want to hand off completely

item.title: Welcome to Efficiencymaxxing
item.description: Plus: Why intent beats volume, a new AI productivity status metric, and a tool for squeezing the most out of cheaper models

item.title: Use Fable Before You Know What to Ask
item.description: Plus: A manual for handing recurring work to Opus, a skill for judging new tools in context, and the specialist model that beat the frontier for less

item.title: A Tale of Two Models
item.description: Plus: Why Anthropic is getting into drug discovery

item.title: Vibe Check: Sonnet 5—A Model Pitched for Everyone Impresses No One
item.description: Anthropic’s new model can draft, code, and analyze competently, but every use case has a cheaper, faster, or smarter alternative

item.title: Claude Code Is the OpenClaw Alternative You Already Have
item.description: Anthropic shipped it over a year ago. Most people just didn't notice.

item.title: The Moral of Fable
item.description: What the most powerful model yet teaches us about the future of knowledge work

item.title: Vibe Check: Fable 5 Is the Best Coding Model in the World
item.description: The Mythos-class model is a warp drive for power users—but overpowered for everyone else

item.title: How Microsoft Is Building for a World of Metered Intelligence
item.description: The company gets pragmatic in AI’s expensive era

item.title: Compound Engineering Gets an Upgrade
item.description: The AI-native engineering philosophy has expanded from four steps to eight

item.title: How to Use Codex for Knowledge Work: A Power User’s Guide
item.description: Setup, workflows, and principles for turning Codex into an operating system for email, writing, research, planning, and reporting—even if you’re not an engineer

item.title: Notes From the Foothills of the Singularity
item.description: Google I/O wasn't flashy, but it might be the most important yet

item.title: We Gave Every Employee an AI Agent. Here's What We're Doing Differently Now.
Socrates as a Service
item.description: The best stories live in people—and getting them out is still a human job

item.title: Claude Code for Product Managers
item.description: How one general manager runs a full product using AI for planning, monitoring, and research

item.title: How I Escaped AI Autopilot
item.description: The more reliable AI gets, the less we check its work. Research explains why, and what to do about it.

item.title: The Folder Is the Agent
item.description: I'm running 44 AI agents across multiple projects. Each one is just a model pointed at a folder.

item.title: How We Run a 25-person Company on Four AI Agents
item.description: How Every uses custom agents for prioritization, meeting notes, OKR planning, and growth tracking—plus sample prompts to build your own

item.title: What I Learned Onboarding Our AI Project Manager
item.description: Claudie saves us 15 hours a week, but getting her up to speed was harder than hiring a human

item.title: The Agent That Saved My Brain
item.description: How Every's growth lead built a command center in Claude Code

item.title: The Science of Why AI Still Can't Write Like You
item.description: New research on writing style reveals that the most distinctive parts of your prose are the ones you don't even think about

item.title: This Is How the Every Editorial Team Uses AI
item.description: We’re publishing our editorial guidelines, plus the workflows our writers, editors, and producers have built around them
